mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the trigger fails to predict drawdowns exceeding 2% in the subsequent month.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of real consumer credit and accelerating inflation indicates a demand shock combined with monetary tightening, which simultaneously crushes corporate margins and raises discount rates, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of constrained industrial production growth, a sudden spike in manufacturing output acts as a supply-side relief valve that eases input cost pressures and supports corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant positive skew when INDPRO spikes during low-growth quarters, or the signal produces false positives in > 40% of supply-constrained episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 100,000 units signals a sharp reversal in residential investment demand, which historically precedes broader economic slowdowns and equity sell-offs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The trigger fails to precede equity drawdowns in > 50% of historical instances, or forward returns remain flat despite repeated trigger activations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust aggregate demand and wage growth, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 18-month rolling window, or positive payroll surprises consistently fail to generate positive equity returns in the subsequent month.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises while real GDP growth decelerates, the combination indicates restrictive monetary policy choking off economic activity, which compresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict negative returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero during concurrent rate hikes and GDP slowdowns.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where unemployment remains below 4.0%, a sudden acceleration in payroll growth signals an overheating labor market that forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate rate hikes, ultimately triggering a risk-off equity correction over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in > 50% of late-cycle instances where payrolls surge, or forward returns remain positive despite the trigger activating.
